seo.title: Krabi: Complete Travel Guide 2026 — Beaches, Islands & Limestone Cliffs
seo.metaDescription: Plan your Krabi trip with our complete 2026 guide. Railay Beach, 4 Islands tour, Tiger Cave Temple, best hotels from budget to luxury, and practical tips for Thailand's Andaman coast.

overview: Krabi is the Andaman coast's most dramatic destination — towering limestone karsts rise straight from turquoise waters, boat-access-only beaches feel like private islands, and world-class rock climbing routes scale sheer cliff faces. Unlike neighboring Phuket, Krabi isn't one island but a province of mainland coastline, offshore islands, and jungle-covered mountains. The main tourist hub of Ao Nang puts you within longtail-boat distance of Railay Beach, the 4 Islands, and Koh Phi Phi, while Krabi Town offers a more authentic Thai experience with its riverside night markets and budget-friendly guesthouses. Whether you're scaling Tiger Cave Temple's 1,237 steps, swimming in jungle emerald pools, or island-hopping by longtail, Krabi delivers raw natural beauty without the overdevelopment of more commercial beach destinations.
highlights: Railay Beach — a boat-access-only peninsula with four interconnected beaches, world-class rock climbing, and dramatic limestone cliffs
Tiger Cave Temple (Wat Tham Suea) — climb 1,237 steps to a summit Buddha statue with 360-degree views over Krabi province, free entrance
4 Islands Tour by longtail boat — Chicken Island, Tup Island, Poda Island, and Phra Nang Cave Beach for 1,000-1,500 THB
Emerald Pool (Sa Morakot) — crystal-clear natural swimming pool in the Khao Pra-Bang Khram Wildlife Sanctuary
Koh Phi Phi day trips — 1.5 hours by speedboat to one of Thailand's most iconic island groups
Krabi Town Walking Street night market — weekend street food, local crafts, and authentic Thai atmosphere at local prices
Hot Spring Waterfall — soak in natural thermal springs surrounded by tropical forest
Ao Nang beachfront — the main tourist hub with restaurants, shops, and boat departures to Railay and the islands
Koh Lanta — a large, laid-back island south of Krabi with long beaches and fewer crowds than Phi Phi
Rock climbing at Railay and Tonsai — over 700 bolted routes on limestone cliffs, from beginner to advanced

bestTimeToVisit.peak: Nov-Feb: Dry season with clear skies, calm seas, and ideal conditions for island hopping, diving, and rock climbing. December-January is busiest with highest hotel prices. February offers equally good weather with slightly fewer crowds.
bestTimeToVisit.shoulder: Mar-Apr: Still dry but increasingly hot (35C+). Fewer tourists than peak season, hotel prices drop 15-30%. Seas remain calm for island tours. Songkran (Thai New Year) in April brings a fun atmosphere.
bestTimeToVisit.lowSeason: May-Oct: Southwest monsoon brings afternoon rain showers and rougher seas. Some island tours and ferry routes to Koh Phi Phi and Koh Lanta are reduced or cancelled. Hotel prices drop 30-50%. Emerald Pool and Tiger Cave Temple are still accessible year-round.
bestTimeToVisit.recommendation: Visit November to February for guaranteed beach weather and full access to all islands and tours. March-April works well for mainland attractions and budget travelers. Avoid booking island tours June-August when seas are roughest — stick to Railay (sheltered) and inland attractions during monsoon.

topAttractions.name: Railay Beach
topAttractions.description: A stunning peninsula accessible only by longtail boat from Ao Nang (100 THB one-way, 15 minutes). Four interconnected beaches — Railay West (the main swimming beach), Railay East (mangroves and accommodation), Phra Nang Cave Beach (dramatic cave with stalactites), and Tonsai (backpacker climbing hub). The limestone cliffs host over 700 bolted rock climbing routes attracting climbers worldwide.
topAttractions.location: Railay Peninsula, 15 minutes by longtail from Ao Nang
topAttractions.entranceFee: Free (longtail boat 100 THB one-way from Ao Nang)
tips: Take a longtail from Ao Nang — boats leave when full (8-10 people) or you can charter one for 800 THB
Walk between all four beaches via jungle trails — Phra Nang Cave Beach is the most spectacular
Half-day rock climbing courses start from 1,000 THB with gear included

topAttractions.name: Tiger Cave Temple (Wat Tham Suea)
topAttractions.description: One of Krabi's most rewarding experiences — a Buddhist temple complex with a famous 1,237-step staircase carved into the cliff face. At the summit, a golden Buddha statue sits on a plateau with breathtaking 360-degree views over Krabi Town, the Andaman Sea, and surrounding limestone mountains. The main cave temple at the base contains monk cells and a meditation area.
tips: Start early morning (before 8 AM) to avoid the midday heat — the stairs have no shade
Bring water and a towel — you will sweat heavily on the climb
The descent is harder on the knees than the ascent — take your time

topAttractions.name: Emerald Pool (Sa Morakot)
topAttractions.description: A natural spring-fed pool with crystal-clear emerald-green water surrounded by lowland rainforest in the Khao Pra-Bang Khram Wildlife Sanctuary. The mineral-rich water maintains a constant warm temperature and the color comes from reflected light and mineral deposits. A short boardwalk trail through the forest leads to the pool.
topAttractions.location: Thung Teao Forest Natural Park, 65 km from Krabi Town
tips: Visit on a weekday morning to avoid tour bus crowds
The nearby Blue Pool is a 1.4 km trail further — even more vivid color but no swimming allowed
Wear water shoes — the pool bottom is slippery

topAttractions.name: 4 Islands Tour
topAttractions.description: A full-day longtail boat tour visiting four stunning locations off the Krabi coast: Chicken Island (named for its chicken-head-shaped rock formation), Tup Island (connected to Chicken Island by a sandbar at low tide), Poda Island (white-sand beach and snorkeling), and Phra Nang Cave Beach (stunning limestone cave on Railay peninsula). Includes snorkeling stops and beach time.
tips: Longtail boat tours are cheaper and more authentic than speedboat versions
Book ahead in peak season — boats fill up quickly
Check the tide table: the sandbar between Chicken and Tup Island is only walkable at low tide

topAttractions.name: Hot Spring Waterfall
topAttractions.description: A series of natural thermal pools and a small waterfall in a lush forest setting. The warm mineral water (35-42C) flows over calcium deposits creating terraced pools perfect for soaking. A unique combination of hot springs and natural waterfall in a jungle setting — unlike anything else in the region.
topAttractions.location: Klong Thom district, 70 km from Krabi Town
tips: Combine with Emerald Pool — they're only 15 minutes apart by car
Best enjoyed in the early morning before tour groups arrive
Wear a swimsuit and bring a change of clothes

topAttractions.name: Ao Nang Beach
topAttractions.description: Krabi's main tourist beach and the gateway to the islands. A long stretch of sand backed by limestone cliffs with a bustling beachfront promenade lined with restaurants, dive shops, and tour operators. While not Krabi's prettiest beach (that's Railay), Ao Nang is the most convenient base for day trips and has the widest selection of accommodation and nightlife.
topAttractions.location: Ao Nang, 20 km from Krabi Town
tips: Walk to the western end for the best swimming — away from the longtail boat traffic
Longtail boats to Railay (100 THB) and 4 Islands tours depart from the eastern end
The Ao Nang night bazaar has decent food at tourist prices — walk one street back for cheaper local places

travelTips: Rent a scooter (200-300 THB/day) to explore beyond Ao Nang — Tiger Cave Temple, Emerald Pool, and Hot Springs are all inland and not easily reached by public transport
Longtail boats from Ao Nang to Railay cost 100 THB and run until about 6 PM — after that you'll need to charter (800 THB)
Krabi Town is significantly cheaper than Ao Nang for food, accommodation, and shopping — consider basing yourself there if on a tight budget
The 4 Islands tour by longtail (1,000-1,500 THB) is better value and more atmospheric than the speedboat version (1,800-2,500 THB)
ATM fees are 220 THB per withdrawal — take out larger amounts or carry cash from Bangkok
Tiger Cave Temple stairs have no shade — bring at least 1 liter of water and start before 8 AM to avoid heatstroke
Ferry connections to Koh Phi Phi (1.5 hrs) and Koh Lanta (2 hrs) run daily in high season but are reduced or cancelled in monsoon months
Book Railay accommodation in advance during peak season (Dec-Jan) — there are limited hotels and the popular ones sell out
Grab and Bolt work in Krabi Town and Ao Nang — use them instead of tuk-tuks for fair prices
International driving permit is technically required for scooter rental — police checkpoints happen occasionally on the Ao Nang to Krabi Town road
gettingThere: Krabi International Airport (KBV) has multiple daily flights from Bangkok — AirAsia, Nok Air, and Thai Lion Air from 1,500 THB one-way (1.5 hours). Airport to Ao Nang is 30 minutes by shared shuttle (150 THB) or private taxi (600 THB). Buses from Bangkok's Southern Terminal take 10-12 hours (from 600 THB). Ferries connect Krabi to Koh Phi Phi (1.5 hrs, 450 THB), Koh Lanta (2 hrs, 400 THB), and Phuket (2.5 hrs, 550 THB).
gettingAround: Within Ao Nang, most things are walkable along the beachfront road. Songthaew (shared pickup trucks) run between Krabi Town and Ao Nang for 60 THB. Longtail boats are the main transport to Railay (100 THB) and the islands. Scooter rental costs 200-300 THB/day and is essential for reaching inland attractions like Tiger Cave Temple, Emerald Pool, and Hot Springs. Tuk-tuks are available but negotiate the price before boarding — expect 300-500 THB for Ao Nang to Krabi Town. Grab and Bolt ride-hailing work in the main areas.
